obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be resilient against errors, faults or inconsistencies that may occur within the system or the environment in which the system operates, in particular due to their interaction with natural persons or other systems. Technical redundancy solutions shall include, where appropriate, backup or fail-safe plans.
scoring_text: Skill in AI system resilience engineering, including error handling design, fault tolerance implementation, environmental interaction robustness, human-AI interaction failure modes, system-to-system interaction resilience, and backup and fail-safe plan design.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI system resilience engineering, including error handling design, fault tolerance implementation, environmental interaction robustness, human-AI interaction failure modes, system-to-system interaction resilience, and backup and fail-safe plan design.
reference_document_element_description: Evaluate AI application performance against defined quality, safety, and reliability criteria, documenting test results and identified failure modes.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 15(3) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AI domain. AI-T-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.568, cosine: 0.628). Secondary: RM-S-002 (RM, cosine: 0.618), RM-T-003 (RM, cosine: 0.608).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.7/10 (raw 0.568). Shared vocabulary: [ai, failure, modes]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [backup, design, engineering, environmental, error, fail] vs WIDAI terms [against, application, criteria, defined, documenting, evaluate] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.866 (P=0.862, R=0.871). Balanced P/R — the 29-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.2%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.628 > STS 0.568 (gap +0.060). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, failure, modes] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, failure, modes]. EU AI Act-only: [backup, design, engineering, environmental, error, fail]. WIDAI-only: [against, application, criteria, defined, documenting, evaluate].
